title: ProcScript: Data Types
html:
ProcScript: Data Types

  • Last UpdatedDez 26, 2024
    • Uniface
    • 10.4

Data types define the characteristics of the data and determine how Uniface handles the data during processing and storage. They are specified for parameters, variables, and return values in ProcScript, as well as for fields, global variables, and component variables.

For more information, refer to the Uniface Data Types topic.

Related Links
  • Uniface Data Types

    Data types are classifications for data containers, such as fields, variables, parameters, and return values. They define the characteristics of the data and determine how the data is handled during processing and storage. Uniface has scalar data types and reference data types.

  • Data Handling in ProcScript

    The ProcScript language enables you to convert data from one type to another, perform arithmetic operations, extract data values, and perform string substitution. Uniface carries out implicit type conversion whenever necessary, for example, when assignments are made between non-matching data types or when comparing non-matching data types. The ProcScript language also provides a number of functions that allow you to convert from one data type to another.

  • Transforming Complex Data Using Structs

    You can dynamically transform complex data structures, such as XML, JSON, or Uniface component structures, into another format using in-memory data structures called Structs.

  • Storage Formats

    DBMS vendors use the term data type to describe the way data is handled and stored on disk, but this invariably means the way the data is actually stored. In Uniface documentation, the term storage format is therefore used to refer to DBMS data types . A Uniface data type is specified for each field in a modeled entity; a DBMS storage format is internal to the DBMS.

  • Packing Codes

    Packing codes are mappings between Uniface data types and DBMS data types (storage formats). When writing a field, Uniface passes the data and a valid packing code to the DBMS connector, which uses the packing code to determine the appropriate storage format for storing the data in the target DBMS. Packing codes also determine how data is interfaced with 3GL routines.
